reference_review:
SUMMARY:
That seems good. See some comments and questions inline.

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/build.properties:7] This seems erroneous, the `build.properties` doesn't need to change.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/pom.xml:135] No need for extra lines here. You can reset to previous state to simplify the change (and the review)
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/AbstractDebugDelegate.java:42] Are all those attributes the same for the 3 debug adapters?
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/RunHTMLDebugTab.java:41] If this class isn't meant to be instantiated directly, make it `abstract` and call it `AbstractRunHTMLDebugTab`
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/AbstractDebugDelegate.java:35] +1, and also, as it's only for HTML, `AbstractHTMLDebugDelegate`.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/node/NodeRunDAPDebugDelegate.java:99] use a lambda here instead of explicit `new Runnable()`

```java
Display.getDefault().asyncExec(() -> ErrorDialog.openError(Display.getDefault().getActiveShell(), "Debug error", e.getMessage(), errorStatus)); //$NON-NLS-1$
```
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/node/NodeAttachDebugDelegate.java:69] use a lambda here instead of explicit `new Runnable()`

```java
Display.getDefault().asyncExec(() -> ErrorDialog.openError(Display.getDefault().getActiveShell(), "Debug error", e.getMessage(), errorStatus)); //$NON-NLS-1$
```

SUMMARY:
In general, the change looks good. But there is at least one potential behavorial change beyond the lazy handle creation in there that we need to clarify or resolve.

POINTS:
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Cursor.java:533] This does not do any "setup" anymore but just retrieves some OS identifier for a cursor style, so method name should be adapted.
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Cursor.java:601] This check (and others in other constructors) now makes the same one inside `setupCursorFromImageData` obsolete as they are already checked here in the provider constructors, so the replicated checks should be removed there.
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Cursor.java:687] This changes the current behavior: now the provider is initialized with a mask retrieved from the source instead of just storing the originally passed mask (which may be null). Maybe this is a cleaner way of doing it but (1) it should then be done independent from this change of making handle creation lazy and (2) we then need to ensure that a mask that is first created from source and then scaled is the same as first scaling the source and then extracting a mask out of it.
- [tests/org.eclipse.swt.tests/JUnit Tests/org/eclipse/swt/tests/junit/Test_org_eclipse_swt_graphics_Cursor.java:172] Parts of the error messages are rather obsolete. In particular the expectation of throwing an IllegalArgumentException is already encoded in the assertions themselves.
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Cursor.java:359] This `destroyHandle()` method could be combined with the `destroy()` method and all the comment/commented-out code could be removed from that method.

POINTS:
- [docs/src/main/sphinx/connector/delta-lake.md:231] It would be clearer if we said `row_group` instead of `block`.
"Block" clashes with the Trino Block that `parquet_max_read_block_size` is about. `parquet_writer_block_size` is an unfortunate hang-over from parquet-java/hadoop terminology, we could change that to `parquet_writer_row_group_size` for consistency.
- [docs/src/main/sphinx/connector/delta-lake.md:232] Should the property be `..._max_row_count` ?
- [plugin/trino-delta-lake/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/deltalake/DeltaLakeSessionProperties.java:154] The maximum row count of row groups created by the Parquet writer
- [plugin/trino-hive/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/hive/parquet/ParquetWriterConfig.java:44] This will be at least as large as a Page, so some `N * PARQUET_WRITER_MIN_PAGE_VALUE_COUNT` might be more reasonable lower bound
- [docs/src/main/sphinx/object-storage/file-formats.md:78] I'm wondering what's the use case for limiting max row count in row groups ? Small row-groups have obvious drawbacks like smaller column chunks for IO and more metadata in the footer. The only benefit is a potential for more granular row-group filtering. But we could get that through page indexes without changing the row-group size (not implemented by Trino writer today, but doable).
I've been thinking that we should have a "min row count" limit, because that helps for the scenario of columns with large strings, or when the number of columns is large. We have had to manually tune row-group sizes higher for wide table scenarios so far.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR, @Chu3laMan. I've left a few comments for you to consider when you have a minute.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/security/AuthenticationAuditListener.java:104] This check isn't needed as `getAuthentication()` should never return `null`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/security/AuthenticationAuditListener.java:108] The username's already included in the event (it's its `principal`) so it doesn't needed to be included as a separate piece of data.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/security/AuthenticationAuditListener.java:61] Please add some javadoc here that describes the constant. It should also be marked with `@since 3.4.0`.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-actuator/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/actuate/security/AuthenticationAuditListener.java:79] Please check the formatting. You can run `./gradlew spring-boot-project:spring-boot-actuator:format` to correct it automatically.

reference_review:
POINTS:
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/ExtensionLoader.java:640] Maybe all three options should be dropped into a single "determine" method.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/ExtensionLoader.java:750] This is a pretty strict limitation, not only on what the step can do but also on the way in which it can do it.  For example this makes it impossible to use `BuildProducer` to optionally produce the item.

TBH I don't understand why the restriction exists.  Consuming a `BOOTSTRAP` config object should only have the distinguishing characteristic that the steps may run right away instead of after the "real" run time config is run.  Any step consuming run time config should already have at least one dependency which would prevent them from running too early.  Any `BOOTSTRAP` consuming step which doesn't produce anything that impacts the run time config by definition is agnostic to when it runs, so if it runs later that's fine.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/ExtensionLoader.java:800] Again returning a value is only one possible way of producing it.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/ExtensionLoader.java:804] This restriction should be unneeded because the dependency sorting should already report the problem if you try to produce something in the wrong phase.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/ExtensionLoader.java:877] Why not?
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/configuration/RunTimeConfigurationGenerator.java:397] Should be `final`.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/configuration/RunTimeConfigurationGenerator.java:409] I think in the future we should have a separate `bootstrapIgnored` as well, so we can log warnings about unrecognized configurations in the bootstrap config file(s) (this is the common case) while also being able to warn about ineffective config in the run time config (for example if a config source tries to configure itself, we can warn that the configuration property may not be given at that time).  This depends on #5548.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/steps/MainClassBuildStep.java:222] I don't think it does, TBH, it entails hopefully nothing more than moving the call to read the config into a build step.  The rest should (?) work itself out naturally.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/steps/MainClassBuildStep.java:229] This seems a bit fragile.  Could we instead try to move the config loading into a build step and see if that simplifies things?
- [core/processor/src/main/java/io/quarkus/annotation/processor/generate_doc/ConfigPhase.java:42] Heh! This bit of code probably should not have been approved - instead the phases probably ought to have been reordered and the default comparison used.... but I guess that can be a fix for another day.

SUMMARY:
Please add copyright headers.
If possible, please add a test.
Maybe consider dropping the NoThreadReciler and stick to IReconciler+IDocumentListener?

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/internal/XMLLanguageServerAPI.java:25] You may call it just LemminxServer
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/internal/XMLLanguageServerAPI.java:12] Please add copyright header.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/internal/lsp/ClosingTagReconciler.java:6] why not a IReconciler?
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/internal/lsp/ClosingTagReconcilingStrategy.java:22] I don't think an IReconcilingStrategy is useful here, why not sticking to a documentListneer (inilined in the IReconciler)?
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/internal/lsp/ClosingTagReconcilingStrategy.java:53] That should work. I believe there are some kind of APIs in LSP4J to allow introspecting a language server to check whether it has the corresponding JsonRequest support, but I don't think that would be simpler and maybe not even better...
So sticking to it is fine as long as it works.
By the way, a test to ensure it works would be welcome.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/internal/lsp/ClosingTagReconcilingStrategy.java:74] One days, when snippets are usable more widely in LSP, we'll extract the LSCompletionProposal stuff that supports snippets as an API so such extension will be able to support it.
Or maybe the snippets will be generalized in LSP first and we'll be able to get rid of this custom operation before we have need for an API in LSP4E!

SUMMARY:
Please also replace any occurrences of  `.addListener(ChannelFutureListener.FIRE_EXCEPTION_ON_FAILURE)` with the use of `ctx.voidPromise()` solution. I realised that I had added `.addListener(ChannelFutureListener.FIRE_EXCEPTION_ON_FAILURE)` in #14713 based on SO answer https://stackoverflow.com/a/54377238 . (I added [a new answer to SO with the voidPromise solution](https://stackoverflow.com/a/75066368).)

REPO: eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.swt PR #2526: GC#DrawImage(image, destX, destY, destWidth, destHeight) now tries to load images at the requested destination size before drawing Changed files (8): bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/cocoa/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/ImageDataAtSizeProvider.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/ImageDataLoader.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/ImageLoader.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/internal/DPIUtil.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/gtk/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/GC.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java Diff (+216/-34): PR DESCRIPTION: The DrawImage API with just destination coordinates now tries to load images at the requested size. If the requested size is not available, it falls back to the closest available size by calculating the appropriate zoom. Images are loadable at the target size if the image is created from an SVG stream or filename, or The image is created with an ImageDataProvider that is ImageDataAtSizeProvider. This commit also introduces the ImageDataAtSizeProvider interface to support dynamic sizing. With this changes we need to test the following 1) The svgs are rendered at custom sizes without blurry scaling (this can be compared with current commit vs master) 2) Drawing images with targetHeight and targetWidth with ImageDataAtSizeProvider (comparison can be made against ImageDataProvider vs ImageDataAtSizeProvider both at this commit) Steps to reproduce:
snippet 1 to check drawing svgs at custom sizes (click to show snippet) **Step 1**: Copy the eclipse.svg to same path where the snippets are and run the below snippet. **Step 2**: Run the snippet package org.eclipse.swt.snippets; ```java package org.eclipse.swt.snippets; import org.eclipse.swt.*; import org.eclipse.swt.custom.*; import org.eclipse.swt.graphics.*; import org.eclipse.swt.layout.*; import org.eclipse.swt.widgets.*; public class Snippet386 { public static void main(String[] args) { Display display = new Display(); Image[] image DIFF: --- bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/cocoa/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java (+5/-7) --- @@ -1818,23 +1818,21 @@ public String toString () { * * @param gc the GC to draw on the resulting image * @param imageData the imageData which is used to draw the scaled Image - * @param width the width of the original image - * @param height the height of the original image - * @param scaleFactor the factor with which the image is supposed to be scaled + * @param width the width to which the image is supposed to be scaled + * @param height the height to which the image is supposed to be scaled * * @noreference This method is not intended to be referenced by clients. */ -public static void drawScaled(GC gc, ImageData imageData, int width, int height, float scaleFactor) { +public static void drawAtSize(GC gc, ImageData imageData, int width, int height) { StrictChecks.runWithStrictChecksDisabled(() -> { Image imageToDraw = new Image(gc.device, (ImageDataProvider) zoom -> imageData); - gc.drawImage(imageToDraw, 0, 0, CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(width), CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(height), + gc.drawImage(imageToDraw, 0, 0, CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(imageData.width), CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(imageData.height), /* * E.g. destWidth here is effectively DPIUtil.autoScaleDown (scaledWidth), but * avoiding rounding errors. Nevertheless, we still have some rounding errors * due to the point-based API GC#drawImage(..). */ - 0, 0, Math.round(CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(width * scaleFactor)), - Math.round(CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(height * scaleFactor))); + 0, 0, Math.round(CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(width)), Math.round(CocoaDPIUtil.pixelToPoint(height))); imageToDraw.dispose(); }); } --- bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/ImageDataAtSizeProvider.java (+49/-0) --- @@ -0,0 +1,49 @@ +/******************************************************************************* + * Copyright (c) 2025 Vector Informatik GmbH and others. + * + * This program and the accompanying materials are made available under the terms of the Eclipse + * Public License 2.0 which accompanies this distribution, and is available at + * https://www.eclipse.org/legal/epl-2.0/ + * + * SPDX-License-Identifier: EPL-2.0 + * + * Contributors: + * Michael Bangas (Vector Informatik GmbH) - initial API and implementation + *******************************************************************************/ +package org.eclipse.swt.graphics; + +/** + * Provides an API that is invoked by SWT when an image needs to be drawn at a + * specified width and height. + *

+ * Client code must implement this interface to supply {@link ImageData} on demand. + *

+ * + * @since 3.132 + * @noreference This class is still experimental API and might be subject to change. + */ +public interface ImageDataAtSizeProvider extends ImageDataProvider { + + /** + * Returns the {@link ImageData} for the given width and height. + * + *

+ * Implementation notes: + *

+ *
    + *
  • Returning null is not permitted. If null is + * returned, SWT will throw an exception when loading the image.
    • + *
  • The returned {@link ImageData} must match the requested width and height + * exactly. Implementations should ensure proper resizing and scaling of the + * image based on the height and width requested
    • + *
+ * + * @param width the desired width of the {@link ImageData} to be returned + * @param height the desired height of the {@link ImageData} to be returned + * @return the {@link ImageData} that exactly matches the requested width and + * height + * @since 3.132 + */ + ImageData getImageData(int width, int height); + +} \ No newline at end of file --- bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/ImageDataLoader.java (+8/-0) --- @@ -45,6 +45,14 @@ public static boolean canLoadAtZoom(String filename, int fileZoom, int targetZoo return ImageLoader.canLoadAtZoom(filename, fileZoom, targetZoom); } + static boolean isDynamicallySizable(String filename) { + return ImageLoader.isDynamicallySizable(filename); + } + + static boolean isDynamicallySizable(InputStream stream) { + return ImageLoader.isDynamicallySizable(stream); + } + public static ElementAtZoom loadByZoom(InputStream stream, int fileZoom, int targetZoom) { List> data = new ImageLoader().loadByZoom(stream, fileZoom, targetZoom); if (data.isEmpty()) SWT.error(SWT.ERROR_INVALID_IMAGE); --- bundle

REPO: eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.swt PR #1026: Expose cancel state of FileDialog and DirectoryDialog Changed files (17): binaries/org.eclipse.swt.cocoa.macosx.aarch64/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, binaries/org.eclipse.swt.cocoa.macosx.x86_64/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, binaries/org.eclipse.swt.gtk.linux.aarch64/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, binaries/org.eclipse.swt.gtk.linux.loongarch64/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, binaries/org.eclipse.swt.gtk.linux.ppc64le/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, binaries/org.eclipse.swt.gtk.linux.x86_64/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, binaries/org.eclipse.swt.win32.win32.x86_64/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT PI/cocoa/org/eclipse/swt/internal/cocoa/OS.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT PI/win32/org/eclipse/swt/internal/win32/OS.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/cocoa/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/DirectoryDialog.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/cocoa/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/FileDialog.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/gtk/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/DirectoryDialog.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/gtk/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/FileDialog.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/DirectoryDialog.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/FileDialog.java, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, bundles/org.eclipse.swt/pom.xml Diff (+232/-30): PR DESCRIPTION: The `FileDialog` and `DirectoryDialog` implementations do currently not provide any way to identify that the dialog was canceled. The dialogs return a null string in case no file or folder was selected, but is not possible to identify whether this was intended (via a cancel action) or if there was some unexpected error while the dialog was open (such as too long file paths on Windows). This change introduces the `openDialog()` method as an alternative to the existing `open()` method. This new method returns an optional String, which is only present if a file or directory was successfully selected. An empty Optional indicates a cancellation by the user and an actual error is realized as an SWTException. The dialogs evaluate the operating system's response code for the system's dialog control to identify the dialogs result state. This PR is a result of the proposal by @laeubi in https://github.com/eclipse-platform/eclipse.platform.swt/pull/1005#issuecomment-1921684747. Supercedes and thus closes #1005. reference_review:
SUMMARY:
Just some suggestions/thoughts ...

POINTS:
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/FileDialogResult.java:9] ```suggestion
public final class FileDialogResult {
```
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/FileDialogResult.java:24] ```suggestion
		ERROR
```
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/FileDialogResult.java:61] ```suggestion
	static FileDialogResult createForError(SWTException exception) {
```

I would simply add a `SWTException` here and allow to query that, implementations can then pass additional details in the exception, it could be printed with a stacktrace to track the location of it and so on
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/common/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/FileDialogResult.java:38] ```suggestion
	public Optional<String> getPath() throws SWTException {
```

this should throw exception when passed in an error state and an empty optional if the operation was canceled, that way one can simply always call this method and don't need to fiddle around will `null` checks.

also "Selected" is superfluous here as there is no other path anyways.
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/widgets/DirectoryDialog.java:143] ```suggestion
public FileDialogResult selectFile() {
```

with the above suggestion about optional one can use

`dialog.selectFile().getPath().ifPresent(path-> ... do something here if not canceled and not an error ...)`

POINTS:
- [pulsar-broker/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/broker/transaction/coordinator/v5/TransactionCoordinatorV5.java:233] could this be replaced by the usage of `java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet()`
- [pulsar-broker/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/broker/transaction/coordinator/v5/TransactionCoordinatorV5.java:200] using a Java record here would be a better solution that using a java.lang.String
- [pulsar-broker/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/broker/transaction/coordinator/v5/TransactionCoordinatorV5.java:225] parsing could be avoided with the use of a Java record
- [pulsar-broker/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/broker/service/ServerCnx.java:3568] Claude Code finding:
The legacy handleEndTxn calls verifyTxnOwnership(txnID) (which checks the principal owns the txn, falling back to a superuser check) before finalizing. The v5 early-return path calls
getTransactionCoordinatorV5().endTransaction(txnID, txnAction) with no principal/ownership check at all. The same applies to the ADD_PARTITION/ADD_SUBSCRIPTION no-ops (legacy verifies
ownership there too).
- [pulsar-broker/src/main/java/org/apache/pulsar/broker/transaction/coordinator/v5/TransactionCoordinatorV5.java:194] It seems that the transaction state is updated before publishing events.
The PR description mentions " Idempotent on retry — a header already in the requested terminal state short-circuits".
If `fanOutEvents` fails, there there a way to retry that later? could the transaction get into a state where the transaction has been updated to a terminal state, but events don't get published?

POINTS:
- [core/trino-spi/src/main/java/io/trino/spi/connector/ConnectorExpressionEvaluator.java:32] why are there two of these ?
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/sql/planner/InternalConnectorExpressionEvaluator.java:1] squash the first 2 commits
- [plugin/trino-iceberg/src/main/java/io/trino/plugin/iceberg/IcebergMetadata.java:538] Avoid adding default constructor, it makes it easier to miss passing in ConnectorExpressionEvaluator
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/sql/planner/iterative/rule/PushPredicateIntoTableScan.java:214] You can use `io.trino.sql.ir.IrExpressions#call` to make `new Call...` usages a bit less verbose
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/sql/planner/iterative/rule/PushPredicateIntoTableScan.java:214] Is this equivalent to the `predicate` before ?
I recall that `connectorExpression` represented only a portion of the overall predicate expression, so I'm wondering if `AND`ing them is just creating redundancies in the expression.
- [core/trino-main/src/main/java/io/trino/sql/planner/iterative/rule/PushPredicateIntoTableScan.java:16] This can squash into previous commit

SUMMARY:
In general the snippet looks good and is really helpful for testing. I have few comments on potentialy flaws and OS-specifics, and some more rather nitpicky ones on code style.

POINTS:
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:223] Why not something like...?
```suggestion
					if (zoom % 50 == 0) {
					   return createSolidColorImageData(16 * zoom / 100, green);
					}
					return null;
```
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:216] Why not define and use `green` also at the other places in the method?
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:158] ```suggestion
```
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:145] Replace such comments with properly named classes/methods (if not already there).
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:151] Please enclose expressions into blocks/braces.
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:54] This should be something optional, as it would only work on Windows.
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:122] This looks rather flaky. Can't you do something like `shell.getMonitor().getZoom()` or the like? And even without that, is `getDeviceZoom()` really correct here or shouldn't it be `getNativeZoom()`?
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:68] Is there a specific reasons for this size? Of course, there is to "correct" size, but the shell is very narrow and high, not even allowing the shell title to be fully displayed. Wouldn't it be better to have more like a quadratic shell with the cursor pattern being centered in the shell instead of using magic values like 300 and 100 in the positioning?
- [examples/org.eclipse.swt.snippets/src/org/eclipse/swt/snippets/Snippet386.java:104] I would be in favor of using one line per information to make it easier to read.
